methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Axelera AI is a Series B-stage semiconductor chip company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable set of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms operating in similar technology spaces, including peers such as Positron AI, Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and DEEPX.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the company's $200M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yields $380M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-or-adjacent-stage comparables produced a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 25.3x across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) was not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $323M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $690M. The width of this range reflects material uncertainty around Axelera's competitive positioning within a crowded frontier-chip segment and the early-stage nature of revenue visibility. Stage-progression modeling anchors conservatively; peer medians reflect market pricing for adjacent-stage chip companies but contain wide dispersal, suggesting heterogeneous risk and capability assessments within the cohort.
